lesson.title: CAPS.Mathematics.Gr11.ALG.2: Solve quadratic equations (factorisation, completing the ...
lesson.explanation: A surd is an irrational number expressed as a root, like √2 or ∛5. Simplify surds by finding perfect square factors: √72 = √(36 × 2) = 6√2. To add/subtract surds, they must have the same radicand: 3√2 + 5√2 = 8√2. Rationalise denominators by multiplying by the conjugate. Exponent laws extend to rational exponents: a^(m/n) = ⁿ√(a^m).

assessment.title: Assessment: Solve quadratic equations (factorisation, completing the square, quadratic formula). Nature of roots (discriminant). Simultaneous equations (one linear, one quadratic). Quadratic inequalities
